etymology.description: 肉(肉) (ròu, "meat") duplicated -> 肉(肉) (ròu, "meat") corrupted to 夕(夕) (xī, "evening") -> 多(夛) duplicated
etymology.interpretation: 夛 is an ancient form of 多(多) (duō, "many"), representing the concept of abundance. Initially depicting two pieces of meat, it evolved into a duplication of 夕(夕) due to the visual similarity between 肉(肉) and 夕(夕). This repetition was understood to signify 'many' similar to how 林(lín, "forest") comes from duplicating 木(mù, "tree; wood").
